value.found_quote: If you deploy a `managed-mcp.json` file, Claude Code loads only the servers that file defines. Users cannot add, modify, or use any other MCP servers, including plugin-provided servers. The file also suppresses claude.ai connectors unless you allow them alongside the managed set. Two other settings can further filter the managed set: `allowedMcpServers` and `deniedMcpServers` apply to managed servers too, so a managed server that doesn't pass them won't load.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Anthropic's Managed MCP configuration feature (`managed-mcp.json`) in detail. It confirms: (1) it deploys a fixed set of MCP servers with exclusive control — "Claude Code loads only the servers that file defines. Users cannot add, modify, or use any other MCP servers"; and (2) allowlists/denylists can filter which servers load — "`allowedMcpServers` and `deniedMcpServers` apply to managed servers too, so a managed server that doesn't pass them won't load." The feature appears to be generally available (active), with specific version requirements noted only for certain sub-features (e.g., `allowAllClaudeAiMcps` requires v2.1.149+).
